analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$77,736 on Apr 24. Momentum stalled below $80K key resistance after failed breakout. Fear & Greed 46 (fear zone, 3rd consecutive session below 50).
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: 46 (Fear zone, 3rd consecutive session below 50)
BTC dominance 58.1% rising — capital rotating into BTC as defensive crypto asset
Spot BTC ETF: 8 consecutive days of inflows totaling $2.1B; cumulative net inflows $58B; total AUM $102B
Strategy (Saylor) bought 34,164 BTC — largest acquisition since late 2024

BTC rally powered mainly by perpetual futures, not spot buying — fragile momentum
Pentagon-backed inflation warning adds macro headwind to $80K breakout attempt

analysis.macro_backdrop: Fed funds 3.50-3.75% (on hold). No major macro releases in next 24h. FOMC Apr 28-29 (hold expected). GDP Q1 2026 advance estimate + PCE both due Apr 30. GDPNow Q1 estimate at 1.2%. Big Tech earnings clustered Apr 29-30. No CPI release until mid-May.
analysis.technical_note: BTC $77,736 — consolidating below $80K psychological resistance. Low volume compression (statistically precedes expansion move within 3-5 sessions). Strong institutional demand (ETF inflows, Saylor mega-buy, Goldman filing) vs. macro uncertainty (FOMC, GDP, geopolitics). Confidence 0.40 = below 0.50 bot threshold — macro filter INACTIVE this cycle.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral: Institutional demand (ETF 8-day inflows $2.1B, Saylor largest buy since 2024, Goldman ETF filing) and Bernstein $150K target offset by Fear & Greed 46, futures-driven (not spot) rally fragility, Pentagon inflation warning, Strait of Hormuz oil risk, and pre-FOMC caution window.
